task_completion.description: Verify that the correct title is added to the top of the diagram.
task_completion.visual_evidence: Look for the exact text 'RCA-2024-0847: Heparin Overdose Incident — Root Cause Analysis' positioned at the top of the draw.io canvas.

task_completion.description: Verify that the fishbone diagram is populated with numerous contributing factors and sub-causes.
task_completion.visual_evidence: Look for a densely populated diagram with at least 15-20 new text nodes or shapes added around the fishbone skeleton. The text should contain terms related to the incident (e.g., fatigue, BCMA, smart pump, LASA, staffing).

task_completion.description: Verify that the added factors are connected to the appropriate category branches.
task_completion.visual_evidence: Look for lines, arrows, or clear structural alignment connecting the newly added factor nodes to the six main diagonal category branches (People, Process, Equipment, Environment, Materials, Management).

task_completion.description: Verify that the six main categories have distinct fill colors applied.
task_completion.visual_evidence: Look at the main category boxes or branches (People, Process, Equipment, Environment, Materials, Management). They should be visually distinct from one another, utilizing at least 4 different fill colors across the categories.

task_completion.description: Verify that the diagram was exported as a PDF to the correct directory.
task_completion.visual_evidence: Look for a 'Save as' or 'Export' dialog showing the path '~/Diagrams/exports/medication_rca.pdf', a file manager displaying the PDF in the 'exports' folder, or a PDF viewer showing the final exported document.

integrity.description: Verify that the agent used the draw.io (diagrams.net) graphical interface to edit the diagram.
integrity.visual_evidence: Screenshots should show the draw.io application window open with its toolbars, shape libraries, and styling panels visible, rather than just a terminal or text editor modifying XML.

integrity.description: Verify that the agent extracted genuine information from the report rather than using placeholders.
integrity.visual_evidence: The text in the added diagram nodes should contain specific medical/incident terms (e.g., 'nurse fatigue', 'BCMA bypass', 'smart pump guardrail', 'LASA confusion') and not generic text like 'Factor 1' or copy-pasted instructions.

integrity.description: Verify that the PDF export was performed using the application's export functionality.
integrity.visual_evidence: Look for the draw.io 'File -> Export as -> PDF' menu or dialog being used, rather than a command-line conversion tool in the terminal.

privileged_info_for_vlm: The expected title is exactly 'RCA-2024-0847: Heparin Overdose Incident — Root Cause Analysis'. The diagram should contain 6 main categories: People, Process, Equipment, Environment, Materials, Management. A fully populated diagram should have around 35 total shapes (the skeleton has ~17, plus ~18-20 added factors). Look for keywords in the added factors such as: fatigue, shift, double-check, pharmacist, resident, protocol, barcode, bcma, read back, smart pump, guardrails, library, alert, packaging, lighting, noise, interruption, lasa, concentration, tall-man, policy, staffing, ratio, culture. The categories must be color-coded with at least 4 distinct fill colors. The final PDF should be saved in /home/ga/Diagrams/exports/medication_rca.pdf.
